sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: τοιόνδε τοι ταρβοῦντες ἐνδίκως σέβας
sections.translation: Stand in just awe of such majesty , and you will have a defense for your land and salvation of your city , such as no man has , either among the Scythians or in Pelops ’ realm . I establish this tribunal , untouched by greed ,

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: αἰδοῖον , ὀξύθυμον , εὑδόντων ὕπερ
sections.translation:
worthy of reverence , quick to anger , awake on behalf of those who sleep , a guardian of the land .
I have prolonged this advice to my citizens for the future ; but now you must rise and take a ballot , and decide the case

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: αἰδουμένους [*] τὸν ὅρκον . εἴρηται λόγος .
sections.translation: under the sacred obligation of your oath . My word has been spoken . The judges rise from their seats and cast their ballots one by one during the following altercation .

sections.label: Section 711
sections.original: καὶ μὴν βαρεῖαν τήνδ ὁμιλίαν χθονὸς
sections.translation: And I counsel you not to dishonor us in any way , since our company can be a burden to your land .

sections.label: Section 713
sections.original: κἄγωγε [*] χρησμοὺς τοὺς ἐμούς τε καὶ Διὸς
sections.translation: And I , for my part , command you to stand in fear of the oracles , both mine and Zeus ’, and not cause them to be unfulfilled .

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: ἀλλ αἱματηρὰ πράγματ οὐ λαχὼν σέβεις ,
sections.translation: Although it is not your office , you have respect for deeds of bloodshed . You will prophesy , dispensing prophecies that are no longer pure .

sections.label: Section 717
sections.original: ἦ καὶ πατήρ τι σφάλλεται βουλευμάτων
sections.translation: Then was my father mistaken in any way in his purposes when Ixion , who first shed blood , was a suppliant ?

sections.label: Section 719
sections.original: λέγεις : ἐγὼ δὲ μὴ τυχοῦσα τῆς δίκης
sections.translation: You do argue ! But if I fail to win the case ,

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: βαρεῖα χώρᾳ τῇδ ὁμιλήσω πάλιν .
sections.translation: I will once more inflict my company on this land as a burden .

sections.label: Section 721
sections.original: ἀλλ ἔν τε τοῖς νέοισι καὶ παλαιτέροις
sections.translation: But you have no honor , among both the younger and the older gods . I will win .

sections.label: Section 723
sections.original: τοιαῦτ ἔδρασας καὶ Φέρητος ἐν δόμοις :
sections.translation: You did such things also in the house of Pheres , when you persuaded the Fates to make mortals free from death . [*]

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: οὔκουν [*] δίκαιον τὸν σέβοντ εὐεργετεῖν ,
sections.translation: Is it not right , then , to do good for a worshipper , especially when he is in need ?

sections.label: Section 727
sections.original: σύ τοι παλαιὰς διανομὰς [*] καταφθίσας
sections.translation: It was you who destroyed the old dispensations when you beguiled the ancient goddesses with wine .

sections.label: Section 729
sections.original: σύ τοι τάχ οὐκ ἔχουσα τῆς δίκης τέλος
sections.translation: Soon , when you have lost the case ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἐμῇ τὸν ἰὸν οὐδὲν ἐχθροῖσιν [*] βαρύν .
sections.translation: you will spit out your venom — no great burden to your enemies . The balloting is now ended .

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: ἐπεὶ καθιππάζει με πρεσβῦτιν νέος ,
sections.translation: Since you , a youth , would ride me down , an old woman , I am waiting to hear the verdict in the case , since I have not decided whether to be angry at the city .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: ἐμὸν τόδ ἔργον , λοισθίαν κρῖναι δίκην :
sections.translation: It is my duty to give the final judgment

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: ψῆφον δ Ὀρέστῃ τήνδ ἐγὼ προσθήσομαι .
sections.translation: and I shall cast my vote for Orestes . For there was no mother who gave me birth ; and in all things , except for marriage , whole-heartedly I am for the male and entirely on the father ’ s side . Therefore , I will not award greater honor to the death of a woman

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ἄνδρα κτανούσης δωμάτων ἐπίσκοπον .
sections.translation:
who killed her husband , the master of the house . Orestes wins , even if the vote comes out equal .
Cast the ballots out of the urns , as quickly as possible , you jurors who have been assigned this task . The ballots are turned out and separated .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: ὦ Φοῖβ Ἄπολλον , πῶς ἀγὼν κριθήσεται ;
sections.translation: O Phoebus Apollo ! How will the trial be decided ?

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ὦ Νὺξ μέλαινα μῆτερ , ἆρ ὁρᾷς τάδε ;
sections.translation: O Night , our dark Mother , do you see this ?

sections.label: Section 746
sections.original: νῦν ἀγχόνης μοι τέρματ , ἢ φάος βλέπειν .
sections.translation: Now I will meet my end by hanging , or I will live .

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: ἡμῖν γὰρ ἔρρειν , ἢ πρόσω τιμὰς νέμειν . [*] [*]
sections.translation: Yes , and we will be ruined , or maintain our honors further .

sections.label: Section 748
sections.original: πεμπάζετ ὀρθῶς ἐκβολὰς ψήφων , ξένοι ,
sections.translation: Correctly count the ballots cast forth , friends , and be in awe of doing wrong in the division of the votes .

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: γνώμης δ ἀπούσης πῆμα γίγνεται [*] μέγα ,
sections.translation: Error of judgment is the source of much distress , and the cast of a single ballot has set upright a house . The ballots are shown to Athena .

sections.label: Section 752
sections.original: ἀνὴρ ὅδ [*] ἐκπέφευγεν αἵματος δίκην :
sections.translation: This man is acquitted on the charge of murder , for the numbers of the casts are equal . Apollo disappears .

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: ὦ Παλλάς , ὦ σώσασα [*] τοὺς ἐμοὺς δόμους ,
sections.translation: I was deprived of a fatherland , and it is you who have given me a home there again . The Hellenes will say , The man is an Argive once again , and lives in his father ’ s heritage , by the grace of Pallas and of Loxias and of that third god , the one who accomplishes everything ,

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ἕκατι , καὶ τοῦ πάντα κραίνοντος τρίτου
sections.translation:
the savior — the one who , having respect for my father ’ s death , saves me , seeing those advocates of my mother .
I will return to my home now , after I swear an oath to this land and to your people [*] for the future and for all time to come ,

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: μήτοι τιν ἄνδρα δεῦρο πρυμνήτην χθονὸς
sections.translation: that no captain of my land will ever come here and bring a well-equipped spear against them . For I myself , then in my grave , will accomplish it by failure without remedy ,

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ὁδοὺς ἀθύμους καὶ παρόρνιθας πόρους
sections.translation: making their marches spiritless and their journeys ill-omened , so that those who violate my present oath will repent their enterprise . But while the straight course is preserved , and they hold in everlasting honor this city of Pallas with their allied spears , I will be the more well-disposed to them .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: καὶ χαῖρε , καὶ σὺ καὶ πολισσοῦχος λεώς :
sections.translation: And so farewell — you and the people who guard your city . May your struggle with your enemies let none escape , bringing you safety and victory with the spear ! Exit .

sections.label: Section 1.778
sections.original: ἰὼ θεοὶ νεώτεροι , παλαιοὺς νόμους
sections.translation: Younger gods , you have ridden down the ancient laws and have taken them from my hands ! [*]

sections.label: Section 1.780
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ἄτιμος ἁ τάλαινα βαρύκοτος
sections.translation: And I — dishonored , unhappy , deeply angry — on this land , alas , I will release venom from my heart , venom in return for my grief , drops that the land cannot endure . From it ,

sections.label: Section 1.785
sections.original: λειχὴν [*] ἄφυλλος , ἄτεκνος ,
sections.translation: a blight that destroys leaves , destroys children — a just return — speeding over the plain , will cast infection on the land to ruin mortals . I groan aloud . What shall I do ? I am mocked by the people .

sections.label: Section 1.790
sections.original: πολίταις ἔπαθον :
sections.translation: What I have suffered is unbearable . Ah , cruel indeed are the wrongs of the daughters of Night , mourning over dishonor !

sections.label: Section 1.793
sections.original: ἐμοὶ πίθεσθε [*] μὴ βαρυστόνως φέρειν .
sections.translation: Be persuaded by me not to bear it with heavy lament .

sections.label: Section 1.795
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ νενίκησθ , ἀλλ ἰσόψηφος δίκη
sections.translation: For you have not been defeated ; the trial resulted fairly in an equal vote , without disgrace to you ; but clear testimony from Zeus was present , and he himself who spoke the oracle himself gave witness that Orestes should not suffer harm for his deed .

sections.label: Section 1.800
sections.original: ὑμεῖς δὲ μὴ θυμοῦσθε μηδὲ τῇδε γῇ
sections.translation: Do not be angry , do not hurl your heavy rage on this land , or cause barrenness , letting loose drops whose savage spirit will devour the seed . For I promise you most sacredly that you will have

sections.label: Section 1.805
sections.original: ἕδρας τε καὶ κευθμῶνας ἐνδίκου χθονὸς
sections.translation: a cavernous sanctuary in a righteous land , where you will sit on shining thrones at your hearths , worshipped with honor by my citizens here .

sections.label: Section 1.808
sections.original: ἰὼ θεοὶ νεώτεροι , παλαιοὺς νόμους
sections.translation: Younger gods , you have ridden down the ancient laws and have taken them from my hands !

sections.label: Section 1.810
sections.original: ἐγὼ δ ἄτιμος ἁ τάλαινα βαρύκοτος
sections.translation: And I — dishonored , unhappy , deeply angry — on this land , alas , I will release venom from my heart , venom in return for my grief , drops that the land cannot endure . From it a blight

sections.label: Section 1.815
sections.original: λειχὴν ἄφυλλος , ἄτεκνος , [*]
sections.translation: that destroys leaves , destroys children — a just return — speeding over the plain , will cast infection on the land to ruin mortals . I groan aloud . What shall I do ? I am mocked by the people .

sections.label: Section 1.820
sections.original: πολίταις ἔπαθον :
sections.translation: What I have suffered is unbearable . Ah , cruel indeed are the wrongs of the daughters of Night , mourning over dishonor !

sections.label: Section 1.823
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστ ἄτιμοι , μηδ ὑπερθύμως ἄγαν
sections.translation: You are not dishonored ; so , although you are goddesses , do not , in excessive rage ,

sections.label: Section 1.825
sections.original: κἀγὼ πέποιθα Ζηνί , καὶ τί δεῖ λέγειν ;
sections.translation: blight past all cure a land of mortals . I also rely on Zeus — what need is there to mention that ?— and I alone of the gods know the keys to the house where his thunderbolt is sealed . But there is no need of that . So yield to my persuasion

sections.label: Section 1.830
sections.original: γλώσσης ματαίας μὴ κβάλῃς ἔπη χθονί , [*]
sections.translation: and do not hurl the words of a reckless tongue against the land , that all things bearing fruit will not prosper . Calm the black wave ’ s bitter anger , since you will receive proud honors and will live with me . And when you have the first-fruits of this great land forever ,

sections.label: Section 1.835
sections.original: θύη πρὸ παίδων καὶ γαμηλίου τέλους
sections.translation: offerings on behalf of children and of marriage rites , you will praise my counsel .

sections.label: Section 2.837
sections.original: ἐμὲ παθεῖν τάδε , φεῦ ,
sections.translation: For me to suffer this , alas ! For me , with ancient wisdom , to live beneath the earth , alas , without honor , unclean !

sections.label: Section 2.840
sections.original: πνέω τοι μένος ἅπαντά [*] τε κότον .
sections.translation: I am breathing fury and utter rage . Oh , oh , the shame of it ! What anguish steals into my breast ! Hear my anger , mother

sections.label: Section 2.845
sections.original: Νύξ : ἀπὸ γάρ με τι-
sections.translation: Night ; for the deceptions of the gods , hard to fight , have deprived me of my ancient honors , bringing me to nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.848
sections.original: ὀργὰς ξυνοίσω σοι : γεραιτέρα γὰρ εἶ .
sections.translation: I will endure your anger , for you are older , and in that respect you are surely wiser than I ;

sections.label: Section 2.850
sections.original: φρονεῖν δὲ κἀμοὶ Ζεὺς ἔδωκεν οὐ κακῶς .
sections.translation: yet Zeus has given me , too , no mean understanding . But as for you , if you go to a foreign land , you will come to love this land — I forewarn you . For time , flowing on , will bring greater honor to these citizens . And you ,

sections.label: Section 2.855
sections.original: ἕδραν ἔχουσα πρὸς δόμοις Ἐρεχθέως
sections.translation: having a seat of honor at the house of Erechtheus , will obtain from hosts of men and women more than you could ever win from other mortals . So do not cast on my realm keen incentives to bloodshed , harmful to young hearts ,

sections.label: Section 2.860
sections.original: νέων , ἀοίνοις [*] ἐμμανεῖς θυμώμασι ,
sections.translation: maddening them with a fury not of wine ; and do not , as if taking the heart out of fighting cocks , plant in my people the spirit of tribal war and boldness against each other . Let their war be with foreign enemies , and without stint for one

sections.label: Section 2.865
sections.original: ἐν ᾧ τις ἔσται [*] δεινὸς εὐκλείας ἔρως :
sections.translation: in whom there will be a terrible passion for glory ; but I say there will be no battling of birds within the home . It is possible for you to choose such things from me : bestowing good , receiving good , well honored in this land that is most beloved to the gods .

sections.label: Section 2.870
sections.original: ἐμὲ παθεῖν τάδε , φεῦ ,
sections.translation: For me to suffer this , alas ! For me , with ancient wisdom , to live beneath the earth , alas , without honor , unclean ! I am breathing fury and utter rage . Oh , oh the shame of it !

sections.label: Section 2.875
sections.original: τίς μ ὑποδύεται , τίς ὀδύνα πλευράς ; [*]
sections.translation: What anguish steals into my breast ! Hear my anger , mother Night ; for the deceptions of the gods , hard to fight , have deprived me of my ancient honors , bringing me to nothing .

sections.label: Section 2.881
sections.original: οὔτοι καμοῦμαί σοι λέγουσα τἀγαθά ,
sections.translation: No , I will not grow tired of telling you about these good things , so you will never be able to say that you , an ancient goddess , were cast out , dishonored and banished , from this land by me , a younger goddess , and by the mortal guardians of my city .

sections.label: Section 2.885
sections.original: ἀλλ εἰ μὲν ἁγνόν ἐστί σοι Πειθοῦς σέβας ,
sections.translation: But if you give holy reverence to Persuasion , the sweetness and charm of my tongue , then you might remain . But if you are not willing to stay , then surely it would be unjust for you to inflict on this city any wrath or rage or harm to the people .

sections.label: Section 2.890
sections.original: ἔξεστι γάρ σοι τῆσδε γαμόρῳ [*] χθονὸς
sections.translation: For it is possible for you to have a share of the land justly , with full honors .
